operation: First, a 2D profile is sketched on the default XY workplane: draw a 3-unit long horizontal line right from the origin, then a line extending 1.7 units in the positive X direction and 3 units in the positive Y direction, then a horizontal line back to X=0. This half-profile is mirrored across the Y axis to create a full symmetric closed base shape. The profile is extruded 2.7 units in the negative Z direction to form a solid prism. Next, the bottom Z face of the prism is selected, followed by its most negative Y edge, to define a new workplane that is rotated 60 degrees around the X axis. The solid is split along this rotated workplane, keeping only the bottom portion of the split to produce the final part.
geometry: This is a symmetric wedge-shaped solid, formed from an extruded symmetric quadrilateral base profile of uniform thickness, with one end truncated by a flat inclined plane. The part features flat side and end faces, with a single slanted angled face on its shorter end, and is symmetric across the plane aligned with the original Y and Z axes.
function: This part is designed as a precision angled shim, spacer, or structural insert for assemblies, providing a specific inclined mating surface and symmetric base for simple alignment and even load distribution between mating components.
